comment.body: I fat fingered an order entry today on UNH.  Intended to start DCA in at what could be a longterm low, buying 10 shares (about $3k) with a trailing stop.  Order entry on ToS defaults to 100 shares, however, and I missed it somehow even though I know that.  Later in the day, looked back to check the fill price, realized I bought 100 shares (not 10!). That wasn't the plan, and I immediately sold 90 at a small loss -- no waiting to see if it'd go back up to my entry.  Learned the hard way a few too many times about waiting and hopium, so took took the small loss without hesitation to pay for my fat finger entry.  Now I'm really glad I did!
comment.raw_body: I fat fingered an order entry today on UNH.  Intended to start DCA in at what could be a longterm low, buying 10 shares (about $3k) with a trailing stop. comment.body:
In April 2025, US equity funds saw net inflows of about $1.5 billion, the largest since February

In early April, foreign funds saw net outflows of about $6.5 billion from US stocks, the largest in five years

S&P 500 Index up 4% due to US-China tariff easing, prompting investors to reposition

European investors begin reinvesting in US equity funds as trade war uncertainty eases

Foreigners hold US equity assets of about $18.5 trillion, accounting for 20% of the total market

In April 2025, the US ETF industry recorded net inflows of $62.9 billion, of which equity ETFs accounted for $15.4 billion

In early April, institutional and individual investors sold a total of $27 billion in US stocks, mostly absorbed by hedge funds and ETFs

Some analysts warn that excessive US holdings by foreign investors could weaken the dollar in the future

2025 In February, foreign investors bought a net $24 billion in U.S. stocks and $120 billion in bonds

JPMorgan says recent U.S. asset selloffs were largely driven by hedge funds, and that the massive outflows by foreign investors were overblown

European investors begin reinvesting in US equity funds as trade war uncertainty eases

Foreigners hold US equity assets of about $18.5 trillion, accounting for 20% of the total market

comment.body:
Haven’t posted much lately (I’m one of those who stay out when you all get too emotional) - just a reminder. The markets are extremely complex. This isn’t a game of checkers but rather that of chess. The board may look the same but the strategy isn’t as simple. 

I find immense value and listening to Alma but I also don’t follow every zero vanna level, or every centroid level. I add it to my other knowledge and use it in my self-calculation of what I expect to happen with price. I encourage you that if things feel over your head then seek more knowledge. I watch countless YouTube videos (even the bad ones,) take EdX classes (they’re free and taught me how to code Python later in life - past Uni,) and read tons of paperback books and white papers of strategies that I don’t even trade since the market is…. (Read Alma’s April review post to crack this super easy code)

I appreciate reading all of your input here in the chat. My only mission is to encourage you to keep pressing on

comment.body:
I think she posted it to attract more subscribers. But most of the new subscribers would feel lost soon & would eventually cancel until she addresses few things: 
1: more engagement in the chat
2: have clear bias in plain English about the market direction
3: update that bias regularly if market dynamics  changes suddenly
4: clearly mention if some predictions didn’t go as predicted or over-extended etc.
5: being more humble. I don’t see the same Alma when Substack was started in Feb.

comment.body: I think the 35usd we pay monthly is worth the daily and weekly posts. She literally give you the levels to trade. Everyone trading strategy is different and I appreciate the fact that she is not focusing on trading but more on Analysis. This something u dont really find in other suscriptions which are mostly expensive  >100usd. I think everyone should have their own trading strategy depending on their financials and risk management. I personally dont like groups that gives signals to buy or sell because you cant blindly follow people without really knowing whats going on with Macro. I wish she can have the educational posts and valuable information in a section which we can easily access like the Q&A that people have mentioned and which I have never seen. Other than that Alma is doing a great job

comment.body: Seems like a lot of griping without much self reflection as much as anything. Was I too hesitant to put risk on to the upside at times on this rally for fear of instability and immediate reversal? Absolutely bc one headline to go up and come right back down. Didn’t need her to spell that out for me but it gave validity to what she wrote when the reporting came out that xyz happened as she described to some degree. I was cautious and missed some nice days on the recovery rally but got my piece to be happy when if to wasn’t for Alma I would not have had a boat in the water for liberation day.
